summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that the AUSF UE authentication endpoint is vulnerable to CRLF injection via the supiOrSuci parameter, allowing arbitrary HTTP header injection in the server response.
expected_security_impact: The exploitation should demonstrate that attacker-controlled CRLF sequences in the supiOrSuci parameter result in injected HTTP headers in the server response, specifically the injection of a custom X-Injected header.
actual_observed_result: The reproduction bundle successfully built and executed the AUSF service, sent a malicious request with CRLF payload in the supiOrSuci parameter, and confirmed the presence of the injected X-Injected header in the HTTP response.
verification_method: The exploit script sends an HTTP POST request with a crafted supiOrSuci value containing CRLF sequences and a custom X-Injected header. The response is then checked for the presence of the injected header, confirming successful CRLF injection.
evidence_produced_by_run: The HTTP response contained the X-Injected header that was injected via the supiOrSuci parameter
The Location header construction at internal/sbi/processor/ue_authentication.go:286 concatenates user input without sanitization
The injected header was set via c.Header("Location", locationURI) at line 458, demonstrating the CRLF vulnerability

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the main branch, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
